summary_one_line: Reach an ATTLIST NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute whose validated value begins with a non-NameChar, so xmlIsDocNameChar fails and the function returns early.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A DTD declaring an attribute of type NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION (so xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal is invoked) whose attribute value's first non-blank character is NOT a doc NameChar, driving the !xmlIsDocNameChar test true and the early return(0) at line 3780.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile/value_profile_cmplog winners resolve the branch (10/10 and 8/10) and hit the true side (W: line 3780 [W], L3779 T=29 F=0), i.e. they synthesise an attribute value whose leading char is rejected by xmlIsDocNameChar; the CMP_MAP gradient over the character-class comparisons inside xmlIsDocNameChar lets value_profile incrementally evolve a byte that lands in the rejected class.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive and cmplog losers stay blocked (8/10) and instead take the false side, falling into the xmlIsDocNameChar acceptance loop at line 3782 onward (L marks 3782-3810, L3779 T=0 F=58); their attribute values keep starting with an accepted NameChar so the true branch is never reached.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile (and value_profile_cmplog) add a CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance buckets; the integer-CMP comparisons that implement xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class test produce new distance buckets as a candidate byte approaches a value outside the accepted NameChar set, preserving intermediate inputs as corpus and letting the fuzzer climb to a rejected leading character. The single-technique delta in both pairs is value_profile (vpc vs cmplog and vp vs naive both add only the CMP_MAP gradient).

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same NMTOKENS/ENUMERATION attribute context, but reached by structurally recombining generalised DTD/element token fragments so the attribute value begins with a non-NameChar; grimoire reaches it far faster (avg blocked 0.10h, hitcount 333) than cmplog.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire winner seeds carry Grimoire structural mutators (mutation_op=GrimoireExtensionMutator, GrimoireRecursiveReplacementMutator, GrimoireRandomDeleteMutator) and are compact restructured DTD inputs; grimoire resolves 10/10 with avg duration blocked 0.10h and hitcount 333, indicating it rapidly assembles the valid DTD+ATTLIST skeleton and varies the attribute-value head structurally.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog loser stays blocked 8/10 at avg duration 19.10h with hitcount 2; it has the cmplog I2S stage but lacks the GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators, so it cannot recombine the DTD token structure to land a non-NameChar leading attribute value within budget.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack (so I2S is held constant between grimoire and cmplog) and ADDS a GeneralizationStage plus Grimoire structural mutators that splice generalised token/gap structures; this grammar-free structural recombination of the DTD/element skeleton is the single-technique delta (grimoire_structural) that produces the qualifying attribute value.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the !xmlIsDocNameChar test in xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
The function is reached only for NMTOKENS or ENUMERATION attribute types via the caller switch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
case XML_ATTRIBUTE_NMTOKENS:
[B]  3875  	case XML_ATTRIBUTE_ENUMERATION:
[B]  3876  	    return(xmlValidateNmtokensValueInternal(doc, value)); <-- CALL

evidence_trail.claim: Winners take the true side (early return 0); losers fall into the acceptance loop.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L3779  T=29 F=0  T=0 F=58  if (!xmlIsDocNameChar(doc, val))  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive and value_profile_cmplog wins over cmplog, both with delta value_profile.
value_profile resolves 8/10 against naive's 2/10 with the value_profile delta.
The value_profile technique adds a CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback that preserves inputs whose operands get closer to a target.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire wins over cmplog with the grimoire_structural single-technique delta.
grimoire resolves the branch almost immediately (0.10h, hitcount 333) versus cmplog (19.10h, hitcount 2).
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
avg duration blocked: winner=0.10h  loser=19.10h
  avg hitcount on branch: winner=333  loser=2

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire builds on the full cmplog stack and adds GeneralizationStage + Grimoire structural mutators, so the single-technique delta vs cmplog is structural.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore
`grimoire` vs `cmplog` (both have I2S; grimoire adds generalization +
Grimoire mutators), not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: grimoire winner seeds carry Grimoire structural mutator ops.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Lineage verification is mandatory only for claimed_mechanism=I2SRandReplace; the two value_profile pairs are attributed to the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S. For corroboration I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7143 --role W --fuzzer grimoire --trial 1 --seed 8f8537181bfef3b5` which showed explicit I2SRandReplace ops and a dash row at depth 4, confirming the grimoire seed also carries I2S (held constant vs cmplog), consistent with attributing the grimoire pair to structural recombination rather than I2S; no lineage signal is required to substantiate the CMP_MAP gradient claim.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose attribute-value first-character rejection is gated by a single equality/character-class CMP that value_profile resolves no faster than naive (no per-execution CMP_MAP distance-bucket progress), so value_profile shows no resolution advantage over naive at this branch.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the value_profile advantage comes from a true CMP_MAP distance gradient over xmlIsDocNameChar's character-class comparisons rather than from incidental faster corpus growth is inferred from mechanism text, not directly observed at the CMP level.
